text.title: AI Marketing Tools Stack 2026 Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules every AI Marketing Tools Stack 2026 application must enforce. These are HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them. Plus a skip-methodology rule for decision-tree leaves that exit the methodology.

rule.statement: Every candidate is assigned exactly one function bucket (GEO/AEO, content, SEO, ads, automation). Cross-bucket tools are scored per-bucket separately.
rule.rationale: Cross-bucket positioning is how vendors hide overlap; per-bucket scoring exposes it.

rule.statement: Decision record names the integration targets and the connection mechanism (native, API, Zapier/n8n) for each.
rule.rationale: Tools that do not connect data are folklore-grade; the stack value comes from integration.

rule.statement: Score whether the tool automates handoff into the next step in the pipeline (yes / partial / no).
rule.rationale: Manual handoffs cap throughput; the score forces honesty about the gap.

rule.statement: Score management overhead 1-5 (1=set-and-forget, 5=daily babysit). >3 requires an explicit owner.
rule.rationale: Hidden management cost is where stacks become expensive.

rule.statement: Decision record carries semver + last_reviewed. Re-score on major version upgrade or quarterly.
rule.rationale: Vendor capabilities shift quickly; old records mislead procurement.

rule.statement: Each record names the contingency tool (alternate vendor) if the chosen one fails.
rule.rationale: Single-vendor lock-in without a contingency is an avoidable risk.

rule.statement: Skip this methodology when the decision tree resolves to a skip branch — preconditions are not met OR an adjacent methodology fits better.
rule.rationale: Skipping cleanly preserves the integrity of the rubric; misapplied methodologies produce false positives downstream.
